note: Harden SOST DEX non-custodial legal framing and client-side anti-scam education. Web / client-side only. ZERO consensus / scripts / src changes. No server-side moderation. No reading of encrypted messages. No logging of private content. (A) Meta description / og / twitter rewritten: removed 'central trading hub' wording, replaced with 'non-custodial bilateral signed-offer interface. Client-side ED25519 signing + X25519 encryption. Encrypted relay. On-chain smart-contract escrow on Ethereum (SOSTEscrow), not SOST-operated escrow.' (B) Hero subtitle clarified: 'AI assistant only helps fill the form; you sign every action yourself.' (C) New non-custodial DEX safety notice banner (amber) with the three NOTs: does not custody, not centralized escrow / broker, does not guarantee. (D) New 'Why SOST DEX does not intervene' section explaining the client-side signing path, the blind relay, the on-chain escrow, and the protocol's inability to reverse / force / verify off-chain. (E) New 'What SOST DEX can and cannot verify' two-column section. Can: structural payload integrity, local key ownership, on-chain tx/capsule hashes, public explorer data, transit MAC. Cannot: off-chain identity, future delivery, private DM agreements, screenshots, third-party social accounts, external payment rails. (F) New 'Honest DEX counterparty vs scammer' two-column pattern table (8 rows each side) specific to DEX context (signed offers, PoPC escrow, AI input). (G) New 'Realistic DEX scam examples' section with five attack patterns: fake PoPC contract, fake DEX mirror URL, advance-fee escrow release, screenshot-as-payment-proof, address substitution after agreement. Each example has pattern + warning signs + safe response. (H) New 'If you have been scammed on the DEX' recovery guide. 8 concrete steps. SOST cannot reverse / recover / force / arbitrate disclosed explicitly. (I) New legal-safe DEX footer disclaimer. (J) NEW website/js/dex-anti-scam-warning.js (local-only, no network call, no logging): hooks into SOSTDEX.processAIInput() to scan the user's typed AI-input text for 10 scam patterns (seed phrase, private key, screen share, advance fee, release first, urgency, guaranteed profit, screenshot proof, lookalike domain, wallet-verification phishing). If a pattern is detected, a local modal warns the user with explicit 'Cancel (recommended)' / 'I understand the risk — proceed anyway' buttons. The warning explicitly states it is generated locally in the browser before encryption and that SOST servers cannot read private trade content. Fails open on any error (never blocks normal use). Multi-step trade composer warning hook is left as a future TODO (the composer is structured/enum-based so the same scam patterns do not naturally appear in its fields).
